source_pr.title: br: stabilize TestConcurrency in pitr_collector_test
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #66961

Problem Summary:
- Fix flaky `br/pkg/restore/snap_client.TestConcurrency` panic: \"Log in goroutine after Test... has completed\".

### What changed and how does it work?

- Refactor `pitrCollectorT.RestoreAFile` to expose an error-returning helper for goroutines (`RestoreAFileWithErr`).
- Make `TestConcurrency` wait for all goroutines, avoid using `require.*` from goroutines, and always unblock the failpoint fence + disable the failpoint via `t.Cleanup`.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  \"No need to test\" reasons -->

Test details:
- Failpoint-enabled run:
  - `make failpoint-enable`
  - `go test -run TestConcurrency -tags=intest,deadlock ./br/pkg/restore/snap_client -count 20`
  - `make failpoint-disable`

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
  * Improved concurrent restoration tests with stronger error propagation, deferred completion callbacks, enhanced synchronization, and explicit cleanup to better validate multi-step restore flows and resource handling.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

root_cause_explanation: The concurrency test was flaky because goroutines continued running after the test finished (and used `require.*` in goroutines), leading to panics like "Log in goroutine after Test... has completed". The PR refactors helpers to return errors, waits for all goroutines, and cleans up failpoints deterministically.
